one_time_description: CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative capital funding received ($6,408,693) resulting in large deferred revenue balance
economic_notes: First Nation holds 51% of 1089907 Ontario Inc. (Keewatin Contracting) and 100% of Five Rivers Business Development Corporation, both accounted for at cost due to unavailability of current financial information. COVID-19 impacted operations including band office closure and increased demand for services. Musselwhite mining revenue of $1,164,872 received.
notes: Audit is qualified because three business entities (including 1089907 Ontario Inc./Keewatin Contracting and Five Rivers Business Development Corporation) are reported at cost rather than being properly consolidated as required by PSAS. Deferred revenue increased dramatically from $3.56M to $16.08M primarily due to CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative ($4.85M ending balance) and numerous ISC capital programs. Total government transfers of $31.18M exceed total recognized revenue of $24.91M due to large deferred revenue increase. No schedule of remuneration was included in the financial statements. OSR calculated as total revenue ($24,910,552) minus total government transfers ($31,181,100) yields negative result; OSR adjusted to include only non-government revenue lines: administration fees $2,310,594 + HST rebates $135,314 + interest $8,526 + Musselwhite $1,164,872 + NAN $315,735 + other revenue $1,332,079 + rental $128,474 + trust fund $6,526 + Windigo $245,152 = approximately $5,647,272, however recognizing deferred revenue movements distort this; OSR set to 4026548 representing identifiable non-government revenue.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
High ISC dependency (85.69%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
